tests.title: Correct build under test
tests.requires: You installed the release intended for this test pass.
tests.expected: The control panel version banner shows the build under test (0.10.2), so every result maps to the right code. A stale install must never masquerade as a bug.

steps.text: Open the control panel (http://127.0.0.1:8765) or the packaged app window.
Confirm the version banner reads 0.10.2 - it must match the installer you just ran.
If it does NOT match, STOP and install the correct build first - results from a stale build are invalid.

tests.title: Stored ships & modules finder: locate, transfer quote, already-here/there, honest miss (issue #67)
tests.requires: `[elite].enabled = true`. Dock at a station with a shipyard AND outfitting first so the game writes StoredShips / StoredModules (data + transfer quotes are as of that last dock). Cross-check figures against the in-game Shipyard / Outfitting transfer screen.
tests.expected: Locating a stored ship/module names its system with the transfer cost+time and copies the system (numbers match the in-game transfer screen); an item at your current station says 'here, no transfer'; being in the item's system triggers the already-there rule (no copy); rundowns group here/elsewhere/in-transit; an unstored item is an honest miss listing what you actually have; before docking this session it asks you to dock first.

steps.text: Locate a stored ship: 'Where's my <ship>?' (parked elsewhere) -> names its system and speaks the transfer cost + time, then copies that system to the clipboard (paste to confirm). Numbers match the in-game transfer screen.
Ship that's here: 'Where's my <ship>?' for one stored at your current station -> 'it's here, no transfer needed' and does NOT copy.
Already-there rule: ask for a stored ship while in the system it's stored in -> says you're already there and does NOT copy.
Stored-fleet rundown: 'What ships do I have in storage?' -> a count plus which are here vs elsewhere (with systems).

Locate a stored module: 'Where's my spare <module>?' (fuel scoop, shield generator, FSD) -> its system + transfer cost/time, copying the system unless you're already there. In-transit items report 'in transit'.
Stored-modules rundown: 'What modules do I have stored?' -> grouped here / elsewhere / in transit.
Honest miss: ask for a ship/module you have NOT stored -> says it doesn't see one and lists what you actually have (never invents a location).
Not seen yet: ask before docking at a shipyard/outfitting this session -> says to dock at one first (no crash).

tests.title: Engineers finder: locate + plot, journal-grounded unlock status, by-module, rundown (issue #65)
tests.requires: `[elite].enabled = true`. Unlock status is read live from the journal EngineerProgress event (written at login); locations/requirements come from a bundled offline table. Log into the game at least once this session so progress has been read.
tests.expected: Naming an engineer speaks their system + base + specialty and copies the system to plot; unlock status matches your real in-game Engineers panel (not a generic answer); a module query lists that module's engineers tagged with your unlock state; the rundown counts unlocked/in-progress/locked; the already-there rule applies; with no progress read it admits so rather than guessing.

steps.text: Locate by name + plot: 'Where is Felicity Farseer?' -> speaks her system (Deciat) and base, what she engineers, and copies Deciat to the clipboard (paste to confirm).

Journal-grounded status: 'How do I unlock The Dweller?' -> your ACTUAL status (unlocked / invited / discovered / not started) plus what's still needed. Compare against the in-game Engineers panel - it should match your real progress.
By module: 'Which engineer upgrades my FSD?' -> lists the FSD engineers (Farseer, Palin, ...), each tagged with whether YOU'VE unlocked them.
Unlock rundown: 'Which engineers have I unlocked?' -> a count plus what's unlocked, in-progress, and still locked - matching the in-game panel.
Already-there rule: ask 'where is ...' an engineer while in that engineer's system -> says you're already there and does NOT copy.
No progress yet: with the game not yet logged in this session, ask 'which engineers have I unlocked?' -> says it hasn't read your progress yet rather than guessing.

tests.title: Blueprint / material sourcing: missing-mat gap, disambiguation, farm plan onto checklist (issue #66)
tests.requires: `[elite].enabled = true`. The material inventory comes from the journal Materials event, written when you load into the game - so launch ED (any ship) before testing.
tests.expected: A blueprint query names the recipe and the materials you're SHORT on (not the full list) with sourcing hints matching your in-game Inventory; grade+blueprint phrasing and name-only/module+grade forms all resolve; an ambiguous module lists candidate blueprints; a satisfied roll says 'you have everything'; 'add these to my checklist' drops one objective per short material; with no Materials read yet it speaks the recipe but admits it hasn't read your mats.

steps.text: Missing-mat gap: 'What do I need for a grade 5 FSD?' -> names the grade-5 Increased Range recipe AND the materials you're short on (not the full list), each with a sourcing hint. Cross-check a couple of counts against in-game Inventory -> Materials.
Grade + blueprint phrasing: 'What am I missing for grade 3 dirty drive tuning?' -> the grade-3 shortfall. Try a name-only form ('increased range') and a module+grade form ('grade 5 FSD').
Disambiguation: 'Grade 5 FSD' alone (several blueprints) -> lists the candidate blueprints and asks which, rather than guessing.
Have-everything path: ask for a low grade whose mats you already hold -> 'You have everything for a roll - nothing to farm.'

Farm plan onto the checklist (the differentiator): after a shortfall, 'Add these to my checklist.' -> one objective per short material (name + count + where to farm). Open the checklist panel and confirm; tick one off.
Honest when blind: with ED not yet loaded (no Materials seen), the recipe is still spoken but COVAS says it hasn't read your materials yet.

tests.title: Body finder: nearest body by type / biological signal, correction-not-invention, already-there (issue #68)
tests.requires: `[bodies].enabled = true` + `[elite].enabled = true`. Nearest single body by type or biological signal over Spansh bodies/search, nearest-first from your current system. Misheard types/genera are validated against a bundled vocabulary. LIVE-VERIFY the Spansh bodies request/result shape here.
tests.expected: A body-type query names a real body + system + distance (+ light-seconds) and copies the system; biological-signal queries return a body listing that species; landable + close-in filters are respected; a nonsense type is corrected, not searched blind; paste yields the body's system; the already-there rule applies; the internet-off case fails soft.

steps.text: Nearest body type (happy path): 'Find the nearest Earth-like world.' -> names a real body + its system + distance (and light-seconds from the star), and says the system was copied. Try 'the closest ammonia world' / 'the nearest water world'.

LIVE-VERIFY the bodies shape: confirm the spoken body / system / distance are real and correct (cross-check on spansh.co.uk/bodies with the same reference system + subtype). A wrong/blank answer or ignored filter means the request/result fields drifted (fix the BODIES spec / parse_bodies in covas/search/categories.py and vocab in covas/search/bodies.py).

Biological signal of type X: 'Find the nearest body with Bacterium signals.' -> nearest body listing a Bacterium species; it confirms the signal. Try a specific species ('...with Bacterium Aurasus') and 'any biological signal'.
Landable + close-in: 'The nearest landable body with Aleoida, close to the star.' -> respects both (landable, low arrival distance). Cross-check on spansh.co.uk/bodies.
Correction, not invention: ask for a nonsense type/biology ('the nearest chocolate planet', 'a body with space whales') -> offers the closest real value or asks again; does NOT run a bogus search.
Plot handoff: after a match, paste (Ctrl-V) into the galaxy-map search box -> it's the body's system; it sets course.
Already-there rule: if the nearest match is a body in your current system, it says you're already there and does NOT copy.
Fail-soft: with the internet briefly off, ask for a body -> a spoken 'couldn't reach the bodies database' note, and the voice loop keeps working (no crash).
